prompt: Which role in a mature SAM operating model is primarily accountable for software compliance governance?
choices: SAM manager or SAM owner with defined policy authority and reporting accountability
Any requester with catalog access
Only service desk agents on night shift
Database administrators for all software publishers
explanation: A clearly accountable SAM leadership role is required to govern policy, controls, and compliance outcomes.

prompt: What is the best way to separate duties between SAM and procurement teams?
choices: Give both teams full admin rights in SAM
Define role-based responsibilities where procurement manages buys and SAM validates compliance position
Allow procurement to edit reconciliation rules ad hoc
Move all SAM decisions to email approvals only
explanation: Separation of duties improves control quality by distinguishing purchasing execution from compliance validation.

prompt: Which SAM role activity most improves data quality for license reconciliation?
choices: Skipping software model normalization until quarter end
Disabling entitlement imports from contracts
Assigning model manager responsibilities for normalization, metric mapping, and publisher hygiene
Allowing end users to edit license metric definitions
explanation: A dedicated model-management role keeps core normalization and licensing metadata accurate for reliable compliance results.

prompt: What is the strongest reason to integrate SAM with PPM/SPM demand planning?
choices: To replace all project financial tracking
To avoid capturing software costs in investment records
To remove governance approvals for new software
To forecast licensing impact of planned initiatives before commitments are finalized
explanation: Linking SAM and portfolio planning enables earlier visibility into licensing cost and compliance impacts of planned work.

prompt: Which integration outcome best indicates SAM and PPM are working effectively together?
choices: Project estimates include validated software cost and entitlement assumptions with fewer renewal surprises
License compliance is reviewed only after project closure
Portfolio records exclude software dependencies
All software requests bypass demand governance
explanation: Effective integration surfaces software cost and compliance assumptions early, reducing late-cycle budget and audit risk.

prompt: Why does SPM governance benefit from clearly defined role separation across portfolio manager, product owner, and finance roles?
choices: It clarifies decision rights, accountability, and control quality across investment lifecycle stages
It allows all roles to approve their own funding exceptions
It removes the need for portfolio review boards
It eliminates dependency management requirements
explanation: Clear role boundaries reduce conflict and improve governance consistency from planning through delivery.

prompt: Which integration with PPM capabilities most improves strategic execution in SPM?
choices: Linking strategy objects to projects and epics with traceable financial and delivery status
Replacing initiative hierarchy with incident categories
Moving all roadmap decisions to spreadsheets
Tracking only completed projects in SPM
explanation: Strategy-to-delivery traceability is essential for governing whether portfolio work is producing expected outcomes.

prompt: A PMO wants stronger role-based governance for portfolio changes. What should be implemented first?
choices: Disable approval workflows for budget updates
Configure role-scoped approvals and audit trails for investment, funding, and scope changes
Allow all project managers to edit fiscal plans without controls
Restrict status updates to annual planning windows
explanation: Role-scoped approval and audit controls are foundational for governed portfolio change management.

prompt: Which anti-pattern most harms PPM and SPM integration value?
choices: Maintaining common taxonomy for investment categories
Using consistent periodic reforecast cadence
Mapping benefits to delivery milestones
Keeping strategic plans disconnected from project financials and capacity data
explanation: Disconnected strategy and delivery data prevents realistic prioritization and weakens portfolio governance.

prompt: Which KPI combination best reflects effective role design and PPM integration in SPM?
choices: Faster governance decisions, lower funding variance, and stronger benefit realization traceability
More manual status updates outside the platform
Higher count of bypassed approval records
Longer cycle time to align strategic goals to active work
explanation: This KPI mix captures governance velocity, financial predictability, and strategy-to-delivery transparency.
